LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THE CONDUCT OF DR. WAITS THAT CONSTITUTES MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE AND ALSO ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT DR. WAITS WAS THE AGENT AND/OR EMPLOYEE OF DIGNITY HEALTH WHEN HE ALLEGEDLY COMMITTED MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE AND WAS ACTING IN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF THAT AGENCY AND EMPLOYMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JUDGE KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kelly Snowden of Kenny, Snowden & Norine telephone number 530-225-8990 for Defendant St. Elizabeth Community Hospital.
Richard Dalrymple of Law Office of Larry S. Buckley telephone number 530-343-3695 for Plaintiff Jill Epperson.

The matter was argued. Upon conlusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. In re: DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Dignity Health's demurrer to the sole cause of action for medical negligence is overruled. Plaintiff adequately alleges the conduct of Dr. Waits that constitutes medical negligence and also adequately alleges that Dr. Waits was the agent and/or employee of Dignity Health when he allegedly committed medical negligence and was acting in the course and scope of that agency and employment. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Kevin Waits, D.O. To Transfer Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Nicole M. Jaffee
Off Calendar Feb-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RICHARD H. DALRYMPLE, III of Law Offices of Larry S. Buckley, Tel No. 530-343-3695 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. NICOLE M. JAFFEE, ESQ. of Donnelly Nelson Depolo & Murray, Tel No. 925-287-8181 appearing for the Defendant Kevin Waits, D.O.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification of denying the motion WITHOUT PREJUDICE, and orders as follows: DEFENDANT KEVIN WAITS, D.O.'S Motion To Transfer Action is denied WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Per Juneau v. Juneau (1941) 45 Cal.App.2d 14, a party bringing a convenience of witnesses motion should provide declarations setting forth: (1) the names of the witnesses; (2) the substance of their expected testimony; (3) whether the witnesses have been deposed or given a statement; (4) the reasons why it would be inconvenient for the witnesses to appear locally; and (5) the reasons why the ends of justice would be promoted by the transfer. In addition, the supporting declarations must contain competent evidence and counsel declarations are often insufficient because they are based on hearsay and conclusions. (Lieppman v. Lieber (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 914, 919; Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Superior Court (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 744.) The court is concerned with the convenience of non-party witnesses. Defendant fails to maintain his evidentiary burden. Defendant relies on the declaration of his counsel with attachments to support the motion. While that declaration lists all of the Tehama County witnesses, there is no direct statement from those witnesses that they do not want to travel to San Francisco. Defendant's motion ignores the Sacramento witnesses. The inconvenience to the Sacramento witnesses to travel to Tehama County is likely equal or greater than their traveling to San Francisco. Nor does defendant explain which adverse witnesses plaintiff is likely to call. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
